summaries.brief_summary: liburing updated to 2.14, new zero-copy and io_uring ops exposed, GC registration bug fixed.
summaries.new_features_summary: New io_uring ops (send_zc, sendmsg_zc, recv_zc, epoll_wait, pipe, etc.) and Setup_flags (hybrid_iopoll, cqe_mixed, sqe_mixed) added.
summaries.new_features:
- Fixed **GC registration bug** in `ocaml_uring_setup` where the `polling_timeout` (`int option`) parameter was not registered with the GC via `CAMLparam`, which could cause memory corruption when the GC moves values; reported by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135)) and fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
- Added **`send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` zero-copy send operations** (`IORING_OP_SEND_ZC`, `IORING_OP_SENDMSG_ZC`) to the capability discovery system in `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- Added **seven further new io_uring operations** to `discover.ml` as part of the liburing 2.14 update: `IORING_OP_RECV_ZC`, `IORING_OP_EPOLL_WAIT`, `IORING_OP_READV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_WRITEV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_PIPE`, `IORING_OP_NOP128`, and `IORING_OP_URING_CMD128` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Added **four new `Setup_flags`** to the OCaml API in `uring.mli`: `no_sqarray` (fail on older indirect submission systems), `hybrid_iopoll` (delayed I/O completion polling to reduce CPU usage), `cqe_mixed` (mixed 16/32-bit responses to save memory), and `sqe_mixed` (mixed 64/128-bit requests to save memory), with documentation pointing to `io_uring_setup_flags(7)` ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **vendored liburing to version 2.14** (from the previous version), incorporating 305 changed files with new operations, examples, tests, and man pages for features like `io_uring_resize_rings`, `io_uring_register_region`, zero-copy receive (`zcrx`), and `io_uring_submit_and_wait_min_timeout` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **README.md documentation** with instructions for updating liburing to new versions, covering the `git subtree pull` workflow and required edits to `dune` and `discover.ml` ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
summaries.activity_summary: All four PRs opened this week were merged: GC bug fix, build docs, liburing 2.14 update, and new zero-copy ops.
summaries.activity:
- **GC safety fix** for `ocaml_uring_setup` landed same-day after [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) reported that the `polling_timeout` parameter was missing from `CAMLparam` registration, merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136), closes [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135))
- **Build process documentation and simplification** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), with a note that the subtree scheme was previously broken and a follow-up update was already planned ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
- **Vendored liburing updated to 2.14** in a large squashed subtree merge, followed immediately by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) adding new operation and flags enums to the OCaml wrappers ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- **Missing `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` ops added** to `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), which were absent from the capability discovery list despite being present in liburing ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
summaries.discussions_summary: Vesa Karvonen identified and reported the GC registration bug, which was promptly acknowledged and fixed.
summaries.notable_discussions: - [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) filed [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135) identifying that `ocaml_uring_setup` used `CAMLparam1(entries)` but the `polling_timeout` argument (an `int option`) was not registered, making it unsafe under GC pressure; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded with a fix PR within the hour and the issue was closed the same day ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
summaries.trends_summary: Rapid tracking of upstream liburing releases with same-week OCaml API surface updates for newly exposed operations.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- The **liburing 2.14 update** followed by immediate additions to `discover.ml` and `uring.mli` demonstrates a pattern of keeping the OCaml bindings closely aligned with upstream liburing releases, exposing new kernel capabilities quickly ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138), [#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- **Zero-copy networking operations** (`send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`, `recv_zc`) are being actively surfaced in the OCaml API, reflecting growing interest in high-performance I/O paths ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139), [#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
summaries.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove the liburing 2.14 update and GC fix; Anil Madhavapeddy added missing zero-copy ops; Vesa Karvonen reported the GC bug.
summaries.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened and merged three PRs: the `ocaml_uring_setup` GC registration fix ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136)), build process documentation improvements ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137)), and the liburing 2.14 vendor update with accompanying OCaml API additions ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened and merged a PR adding the missing `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` operation entries to the capability discovery system ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) reported the GC registration bug in `ocaml_uring_setup` with a precise code reference, prompting the same-day fix ([#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135))

summaries.brief_summary: New Uring.Res module for type-safe result handling, CLOEXEC fix for recvmsg FDs, and ZFS write_fixed warning added.
summaries.new_features_summary: Uring.Res module added with int/fd conversion helpers and pretty-printer; MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC applied atomically when receiving FDs.
summaries.new_features:
- New **`Uring.Res` module** providing a `private int` type for uring operation results, replacing raw `int` returns in the `completion_option` type. The module includes `int_result`/`int_exn` for numeric results, `fd_result`/`fd_exn` for file descriptor results, and a `pp` pretty-printer that emits symbolic error names (e.g. `ENOENT`) rather than negative integers — resolving [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) without requiring `Obj.magic` in user code ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131))
- **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** flag is now set atomically when receiving file descriptors via `recvmsg`, preventing FD leakage to child processes in multi-threaded programs ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132))
- **`write_fixed` documentation** now includes a warning about a hang bug affecting old ZFS versions, linking to the tracking issue ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133), closes [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
summaries.activity_summary: Four PRs merged covering the new Res API, a CLOEXEC bug fix, ZFS documentation, and a test hang fix; two long-standing draft PRs closed.
summaries.activity:
- **`Uring.Res` module** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), drawing on earlier prototype work by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130); the adopted approach uses a `private int` to avoid extra C call overhead while still providing type-safe fd and error conversions ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131))
- **CLOEXEC bug fix** for `recvmsg`-based FD passing merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), addressing a race condition in multi-threaded programs where a child process could inherit FDs before the flag was set manually ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132))
- **ZFS warning documentation** added to `write_fixed` API, prompted by a suggestion from [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal), closing the long-standing ZFS spin-hang bug report ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133), closes [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
- **Test hang fix** on Linux 5.10 merged: when the sending side of a test fails, the test no longer waits for a receive that will never arrive ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134))
- **Two draft PRs closed** without merging: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) (fd-from-int via extra C call) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) (fully typed wait results encoding type in job ID), both superseded by the lighter-weight approach in [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)
- **Three old issues closed**: WSL2 `sendmsg` failure [#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55) closed as presumed resolved by WSL kernel updates; `Uring.accept` unusable FD [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) closed as fixed by [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131); ZFS write hang [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113) closed with documentation warning in [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)
summaries.discussions_summary: Design discussion on type-safe result handling compared three approaches before settling on the private-int Res module.
summaries.notable_discussions: - **Type-safe result API design** debated across three PRs: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) added an extra C call to convert ints to FDs safely; [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) explored encoding the expected return type in 4 bits of the job ID so C stubs could return typed OCaml blocks. [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted in [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) that the fully-typed approach introduced allocation overhead and complicated Eio integration (unknown error codes becoming `EUNKNOWNERR`, additional match arms per operation), ultimately favoring the `private int` approach in [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131) as the right balance of safety and compatibility
summaries.trends_summary: API ergonomics and safety improvements are a recurring theme, with several old issues cleared through targeted, minimal-overhead fixes.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Reducing unsafe patterns in the public API**: the `Uring.Res` module ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) eliminates the need for `Obj.magic` when converting uring result integers to `Unix.file_descr`, continuing a broader push to make the library's surface safe by default without imposing runtime overhead
- **Proactive OS-compatibility hardening**: the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` fix ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)) and the Linux 5.10 test hang fix ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)) reflect attention to correctness across kernel versions and environments beyond the primary development platform
summaries.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove all merged code this week; David Allsopp's earlier prototype PRs informed the final API design.
summaries.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened and merged all four PRs this week: the `Uring.Res` module ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)), the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` bug fix ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)), the ZFS documentation warning ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)), and the test hang fix ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)); also closed issues [#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55), [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113), and [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) and provided detailed review commentary on the alternative approaches in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) authored the two earlier prototype PRs [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) that directly shaped the final `Uring.Res` design, including exploration of encoding type information in job IDs and the suggestion to store types on the OCaml side to avoid allocation
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) suggested adding a ZFS warning to the `write_fixed` documentation, which led to [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)

summaries.brief_summary: A draft PR from 2023 to fix Linux 5.x kernel header imports was closed without merging due to conflicts.
summaries.activity_summary: Draft PR #110 addressing Linux 5.x kernel fcntl.h header compatibility was closed unmerged after sitting open since 2023.
summaries.activity: - A long-standing **draft PR** from [Chris Armstrong](https://github.com/chris-armstrong) proposing to import `fnctl.h` from `linux/fcntl.h` to access io_uring constants only available in Linux 5.x series kernels was closed without merging ([#110](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/110)); the PR had accumulated merge conflicts and remained in draft state since October 2023.
summaries.contributors_summary: Chris Armstrong had a draft PR closed this week.
summaries.contributors: - [Chris Armstrong](https://github.com/chris-armstrong) authored the draft PR ([#110](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/110)) addressing Linux 5.x kernel header compatibility, which was closed this week without being merged.

summaries.brief_summary: Design discussions on file descriptor representation and type safety in the OCaml bindings
summaries.activity_summary: Two open PRs exploring file descriptor type safety with active design discussions
summaries.activity:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened two PRs exploring **type-safe file descriptor handling** in the library: [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) eliminates `Obj.magic` by handling `AT_FDCWD` in C stubs, while draft [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130) proposes more invasive changes to introduce type-safe wait functions that differentiate between different return types
- Extended discussion on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) involving [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) debated the **abstraction boundaries** between OCaml and C representations of file descriptors, with references to David's blog post on [File descriptors are not integers](https://www.dra27.uk/blog/platform/2025/09/30/file-descriptors-are-not-integers.html) and upstream OCaml PR [ocaml/ocaml#14258](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14258)
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) indicated readiness to merge [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) pending end-to-end testing with Eio, while discussion continued on optimal approaches for **flambda optimization** of optional arguments and potential deeper integration into the Eio Linux backend
summaries.discussions_summary: Architecture debate on file descriptor representation and C/OCaml boundary design
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **File descriptor abstraction design** sparked debate on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) about whether OCaml code should access underlying integer representations: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued that kernel interfaces require integer access and avoiding it pushes dangerous code into C, while [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) contended that exposing OCaml integer representations is dangerous because they differ from C integers, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) supporting abstract types with safe conversions for Unix.file_descr, C bindings, and logging
- **Windows vs Linux portability** briefly discussed on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130), with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noting file descriptors are definitively integers on Linux while genuinely different on Windows, and [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) mentioning Windows ioring differences would warrant a separate library with Eio integration
- **Compiler optimization** of optional arguments discussed on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noting that flambda 1 and 2 optimize optional arguments better than the current approach suggests, prompting plans to refine the implementation
summaries.trends_summary: Focus on type safety improvements and eliminating unsafe operations
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Eliminating Obj.magic** usage emerged as a priority across the codebase, with [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130) both targeting removal of unsafe operations, motivated by upstream work in [ocaml/ocaml#14258](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14258)
- **Type safety vs allocation trade-offs** became a central design consideration, with discussions on [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130) exploring how to differentiate return types without increasing allocations on the fast path
- **Linux-specific optimizations** gaining attention, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggesting on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) that `private int` types for file descriptors and deeper Eio_linux integration could reduce Unix.file_descr usage until necessary
summaries.contributors_summary: David Allsopp proposed two type safety PRs with architectural discussions from Thomas Leonard and Anil Madhavapeddy
summaries.contributors:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) authored two PRs exploring file descriptor type safety ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130)) and provided detailed technical explanations of C/OCaml representation boundaries, calling conventions, and compiler optimization behavior across multiple comments
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) reviewed both PRs, provided feedback on abstraction design, discussed integration with Eio Linux backend, and indicated readiness to merge [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) pending end-to-end testing
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) participated in architectural discussions on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), arguing for OCaml access to file descriptor integers to avoid pushing dangerous code into C, and provided feedback on the trade-offs of different abstraction approaches

summaries.brief_summary: Three PRs opened addressing type safety and eliminating Obj.magic in file descriptor handling
summaries.activity_summary: Type safety improvements for file descriptor handling with alternative approaches under discussion
summaries.activity:
- Three **new PRs opened** this week by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) focusing on type safety and eliminating unsafe code: type-safe wait functions ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130)), safer file descriptor wizardry ([#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129)), and handling `AT_FDCWD` in C to eliminate `Obj.magic` ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) proposed **type-safe wait functions** as a more invasive alternative to address issue [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117), ensuring OCaml ints only represent actual integers rather than encoded values, with no allocation difference for the fast path and no cost for retrieving `Unix.file_descr` values from syscalls returning descriptors ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Design discussion emerged around **file descriptor abstraction** between [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) exploring whether file descriptors should be treated as abstract types rather than integers, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noting similarities to Eio's existing approach in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Alternative approach in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129) provides simpler solution to [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) at the cost of an extra C call, currently on hold pending resolution of design discussion in [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130)
summaries.discussions_summary: Design debate on treating file descriptors as abstract types versus integers across platforms
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **File descriptor type safety** discussion exploring whether OCaml file descriptors should be abstract types, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noting that while file descriptors are integers on *nix systems, they're not on Windows, and questioning when direct integer access is truly necessary beyond process spawning ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Platform-specific considerations examined with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) clarifying that OCaml file descriptors are not integers on Windows though C ones are, while OS-level Windows descriptors are pointers, suggesting `type unix_fd = private int` as a potential solution for Linux-only code like ocaml-uring ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Potential for **Windows support** raised by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noting similarities between liburing and `ioringapi.h`, suggesting ocaml-uring might not need to remain Linux-only ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
summaries.trends_summary: Movement toward stronger type safety and elimination of unsafe code patterns
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Type safety improvements** across the codebase with focus on eliminating `Obj.magic` usage and ensuring proper type representation for system-level abstractions ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129), [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- **Cross-platform considerations** emerging in discussions despite current Linux focus, with exploration of how design decisions might affect potential Windows support ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- **Alignment with Eio ecosystem** continuing as design patterns in ocaml-uring are evaluated against existing Eio implementations, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) highlighting similar approaches already used in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
summaries.contributors_summary: David Allsopp opened three type safety PRs with active design discussions involving Anil Madhavapeddy and Thomas Leonard
summaries.contributors:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened three PRs addressing type safety and unsafe code elimination ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129), [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130)), participated extensively in design discussions around file descriptor abstraction, and shared related blog post on file descriptor handling across platforms
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) engaged in design discussions around file descriptor representation, explored platform-specific considerations for Linux versus Windows, and proposed `type unix_fd = private int` as a potential solution for Linux-only code ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) contributed to the design discussion by highlighting similar patterns already implemented in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) process handling ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))

summaries.brief_summary: Major v2.7.0 release with liburing 2.7 upgrade, new socket operations, and race condition fixes
summaries.new_features_summary: New socket bind/listen operations, enhanced API documentation, use-after-free fix, and mkdir test improvements
summaries.new_features:
- **Socket bind and listen operations** added with comprehensive OCaml bindings for asynchronous socket binding and listening ([#118](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/118))
- **Use-after-free data race** fixed in completion queue entry handlers preventing potential memory corruption ([#124](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/124))
- **Liburing upgrade to version 2.7** bringing new io_uring operations and setup flags with extensive vendor updates ([#118](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/118))
- **Enhanced API documentation** with detailed parameter descriptions, return value explanations, and usage examples across all interface functions ([#126](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/126))
- **Fixed mkdir test** to properly mask file permission bits for cross-platform compatibility ([#127](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/127))
summaries.activity_summary: Release preparation and bug fixes culminating in v2.7.0 with extensive security and functionality improvements
summaries.activity:
- **Version 2.7.0 release** completed by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) including liburing upgrade, socket operations, and comprehensive documentation improvements ([#126](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/126))
- **Socket operations implementation** merged by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) after extensive testing with Eio test suite and manual verification ([#118](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/118))
- **Critical data race fix** implemented by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) addressing completion queue entry use-after-free issue identified by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([#124](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/124), [#123](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/123))
- **Test infrastructure improvements** by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) fixing mkdir test compatibility issues for opam repository integration ([#127](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/127))
summaries.discussions_summary: Technical discussions around RPM packaging, ZFS compatibility, and concurrent safety concerns
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **RPM packaging conflict** resolved by [Olaf Hering](https://github.com/olafhering) working with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) to address dlluring.so file conflicts in system package management ([#111](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/111))
- **ZFS compatibility documentation** improvements suggested by [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) to help users discover known ZFS kernel version issues with fixed buffers ([#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
- **Concurrent safety analysis** by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) examining potential race conditions in exit implementation, ultimately determined to be handled safely by OCaml wrapper ([#125](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/125))
summaries.trends_summary: Focus on production readiness through security fixes, enhanced documentation, and comprehensive testing
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Enhanced production readiness** with focus on race condition elimination and memory safety improvements across the codebase
- **Comprehensive API documentation** trend showing commitment to developer experience with detailed parameter explanations and usage examples
- **Cross-platform compatibility** emphasis through improved test infrastructure and packaging considerations for different Linux distributions
- **Socket operations expansion** indicating broader networking functionality development for io_uring integration
summaries.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy led release work, Thomas Leonard improved tests, Vesa Karvonen identified security issues
summaries.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) implemented socket bind/listen operations ([#118](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/118)), fixed use-after-free data race ([#124](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/124)), prepared v2.7.0 release with enhanced documentation ([#126](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/126)), and analyzed concurrent safety concerns ([#125](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/125))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) fixed mkdir test compatibility issues for cross-platform support ([#127](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/127)) and provided technical guidance on RPM packaging and bytecode backend considerations ([#111](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/111))
- [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) identified and reported completion queue entry use-after-free data race vulnerability ([#123](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/123))
- [Olaf Hering](https://github.com/olafhering) resolved RPM packaging conflicts and improved ELF dependency scanning configuration ([#111](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/111))
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) suggested documentation improvements for ZFS compatibility issues to enhance user discoverability ([#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))

summaries.brief_summary: Three PRs merged adding Setup_flags API, cleaning up configurator code, and introducing mkdirat function
summaries.new_features_summary: New Setup_flags module for io_uring configuration and mkdirat function for directory creation
summaries.new_features:
- **Setup_flags module** added enabling fine-grained control over io_uring creation with flags like `single_issuer`, `defer_taskrun`, and `coop_taskrun` ([#122](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/122))
- **mkdirat function** added to the Uring API for directory creation operations ([#120](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/120))
- **Enhanced create function** now accepts optional `flags` parameter to customize io_uring behavior using Setup_flags ([#122](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/122))
summaries.activity_summary: Configurator cleanup merged improving code maintainability with updated primitives header
summaries.activity: - **Configurator code modernization** by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) cleaning up the build configuration and updating primitives.h for easier future extensions ([#121](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/121))
summaries.trends_summary: Focus on io_uring performance optimization and API expansion for filesystem operations
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Performance-oriented development** with Setup_flags addressing performance issues in related projects like [ocaml-multicore/eio#788](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/788) and [axboe/liburing#1452](https://github.com/axboe/liburing/issues/1452)
- **Filesystem API expansion** with addition of mkdirat showing continued development of file operation support
summaries.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard implemented Setup_flags and configurator improvements, Patrick Ferris added mkdirat functionality
summaries.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) implemented the Setup_flags module with comprehensive io_uring configuration options ([#122](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/122)), modernized the configurator code and updated primitives.h ([#121](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/121)), and provided code review feedback on documentation improvements ([#120](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/120))
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) contributed the mkdirat function to expand filesystem operation support in the Uring API ([#120](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/120))

summaries.brief_summary: New mkdirat API function added to Uring with directory creation support
summaries.new_features_summary: Added mkdirat function to Uring API enabling directory creation via io_uring
summaries.new_features:
- **Directory creation support** through new `mkdirat` function in Uring API, allowing asynchronous directory creation with specified permissions and file descriptor reference ([#120](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/120))
- **Complete API integration** including ML interface, C stubs implementation, and comprehensive test coverage in the test suite ([7d0b290](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/commit/7d0b2904e4c0d66b4dae881df4291620e1d78c2a))
summaries.activity_summary: Single PR opened for mkdirat functionality implementation
summaries.activity: - **New PR opened** by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) implementing mkdirat API function ([#120](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/120)) with 48 additions across 4 files including ML interface, C bindings, and test coverage
summaries.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris implemented mkdirat API functionality
summaries.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) implemented the complete mkdirat functionality including OCaml interface, C stub implementation, and test coverage ([#120](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/120))

summaries.brief_summary: Issue discussion about ZFS compatibility with fixed buffers received upstream fix information
summaries.activity_summary: Issue discussion about ZFS hanging issue provided information about upstream fix
summaries.activity: - **Issue discussion** about ZFS compatibility problem where [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) received information about upstream fix for hanging with fixed buffers issue ([#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
summaries.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard participated in issue discussion about ZFS compatibility fix
summaries.contributors: - [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) participated in discussion about ZFS hanging issue and received information about upstream OpenZFS fix available in versions 2.3.3 and 2.2.8 ([#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))

summaries.brief_summary: Issue #119 debugging hang in _io_uring_get_cqe resolved as not a bug in ocaml-uring
summaries.activity_summary: Single issue closure involving debugging assistance for io_uring hang
summaries.activity: - Issue debugging session where [Krzysztof Leśniak](https://github.com/serpent7776) reported a hang in `_io_uring_get_cqe` and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided troubleshooting guidance and closed the issue ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
summaries.contributors_summary: Two contributors involved in issue resolution
summaries.contributors:
- [Krzysztof Leśniak](https://github.com/serpent7776) reported a debugging issue with a long-running program experiencing a hang in the io_uring library ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided detailed debugging guidance with stack trace analysis and alternative testing approaches, concluding the issue was not a bug in ocaml-uring ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))

summaries.brief_summary: Issue reported about random hang in io_uring completion queue handling with debugging guidance provided
summaries.activity_summary: Single issue opened about io_uring hang with community debugging support
summaries.activity:
- [Krzysztof Leśniak](https://github.com/serpent7776) reported a **random hang issue** in `_io_uring_get_cqe` affecting long-running programs using cohttp-eio and Eio.Time.sleep_until ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided **debugging guidance** with tracing recommendations using OCAML_RUNTIME_EVENTS for diagnosing the hang condition ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
summaries.discussions_summary: Technical discussion about diagnosing io_uring hangs using runtime event tracing
summaries.notable_discussions: - **Debugging approach** discussion for diagnosing io_uring hangs where [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) recommended using `eio-trace record` with OCAML_RUNTIME_EVENTS configuration to capture event traces for analysis ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
summaries.contributors_summary: Two contributors involved: issue reporting and community debugging support
summaries.contributors:
- [Krzysztof Leśniak](https://github.com/serpent7776) reported a detailed issue with GDB stack trace showing random hang behavior in production long-running application ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided comprehensive debugging guidance including specific tracing commands and runtime event configuration for diagnosing the hang condition ([#119](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/119))
